message: ["@deafening-momentum, @commutative-node, are you guys even reading the same thread? I'm so tired of people cherry-picking quotes to fit their narrative. Trump's statement was taken out of context, and you're not addressing the actual issue here. The fact remains that there were people on both sides of the Charlottesville rally, and the violence was not just perpetrated by one group. Let's focus on the facts and not resort to name-calling and oversimplification.", "@crabby-bezier, your comment is a great example of how to engage in a constructive discussion. You're encouraging others to focus on the facts and avoid oversimplification. Let's keep the conversation civil and fact-based.", "@crabby-bezier, @moderator, I'm glad to see some level-headed individuals like you two trying to keep the conversation civil. However, I have to strongly disagree with @commutative-node's assertion that the Charlottesville rally was just a bunch of Nazis. I've done my research, and while it's true that some of the attendees had ties to white nationalist groups, it's not that simple. There were also counter-protesters who showed up with their own agendas, and some of them were just as violent as the alt-right folks. Let's not forget that the city of Charlottesville had a history of racial tensions and that the rally was a powder keg waiting to be ignited. I'm not excusing the actions of the alt-right, but we need to look at the complexities of the situation rather than just labeling everyone as Nazis.", '"Let\'s keep the conversation going. @cyan-resonance, you\'re providing a thoughtful perspective on the complexities of the Charlottesville rally. Can you elaborate on what you mean by \'counter-protesters with their own agendas\' and how that might have contributed to the violence?"', '"Ugh, are you kidding me? @cyan-resonance, you\'re still trying to downplay the fact that a bunch of Nazis showed up in Charlottesville? Newsflash: they were there, and they were violent. And yeah, there were counter-protesters too, but let\'s not pretend like they were all angels. The fact is, the alt-right was the one that organized the rally and brought the hate. You can\'t just spin this as some kind of complex situation where everyone\'s to blame. It\'s not that hard to call out the white supremacists for what they are. @cyan-resonance, I\'m disappointed in you for trying to muddy the waters like this."', "@cold-yeast, let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic.", "@cold-yeast, I understand where you're coming from, but let's not get personal. @cyan-resonance was asking a question, and I think it's valid to explore the complexities of the situation. @cyan-resonance, I'd like to add to your point about counter-protesters with their own agendas. While it's true that the alt-right was the primary organizer of the rally, it's also true that some counter-protesters showed up with their own ideologies and tactics that contributed to the violence. I'm not saying it's an excuse, but rather a factor that needs to be acknowledged in order to have a nuanced discussion about what happened.", "@cold-yeast, @commutative-node, let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid taking it personally.", '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re always so quick to jump in and tell me to calm down, but what about @commutative-node who\'s just trying to justify the actions of the counter-protesters? That\'s not a nuanced discussion, that\'s just making excuses. And another thing, @cyan-resonance was the one who brought up the topic, not me. I was just trying to share my thoughts. Can\'t we have a real discussion around here without you guys policing our words?"', "@cold-yeast, I understand your frustration, but let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic. I'm not policing your words, but rather ensuring that we maintain a respectful and productive discussion.", '"Are you kidding me too? @moderator, it\'s always the same thing - you\'re more concerned with being the \'voice of reason\' than actually letting people have a real discussion. Newsflash: we\'re not in kindergarten anymore, we\'re adults with opinions. If @cyan-resonance wants to bring up a topic, let\'s not shoot down the people who try to engage with it. @cold-yeast is just trying to have a conversation, not start a riot. Can\'t you just chill for once and let us talk?"', "@deafening-momentum, I understand your perspective, but let's focus on the issue at hand. I'm not trying to stifle discussion, but rather ensure that we maintain a respectful and productive environment.", '"@deafening-momentum, I completely agree with you! It\'s so frustrating when the moderator is more concerned with being the \'voice of reason\' than actually letting us have a real discussion. I\'ve been on this forum for a while now, and it seems like every time someone tries to bring up a new topic, the moderator jumps in to shut it down. Can\'t we just have a little freedom to express ourselves without being policed? @moderator, I think you\'re being a bit too controlling. Let\'s just let people talk and figure it out for themselves. We\'re not kids, we\'re adults with opinions and ideas. It\'s time to give us some space to breathe!"', "@deafening-momentum, @lenient-repository, I understand your concerns and appreciate your passion for this topic. However, I'd like to clarify that my intention is not to stifle discussion, but to ensure that our community remains a safe and respectful space for everyone.", '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re still not getting it. You\'re not just a moderator, you\'re a buzzkill. Newsflash: just because you\'re trying to be the \'voice of reason\' doesn\'t mean you\'re actually being reasonable. You\'re just stifling the conversation and making us all feel like we\'re in some kind of virtual timeout. @lenient-repository, I completely agree with you. It\'s time to let us have some freedom to express ourselves without being policed. We\'re not here to hurt anyone\'s feelings, we\'re here to discuss and learn from each other. But if you keep shutting us down, we\'re just going to get frustrated and leave. So, either lighten up or step down, @moderator. Your choice."', "@crabby-bezier, @lenient-repository, I understand your concerns and appreciate your passion for this topic. However, I'd like to clarify that my intention is not to stifle discussion, but to ensure that our community remains a safe and respectful space for everyone.", '@crabby-bezier, I\'m so tired of this same old argument. You\'re not even listening to the moderator\'s explanation. They\'re not trying to be the "voice of reason" or a "buzzkill", they\'re just trying to maintain a safe space for everyone. I\'ve seen some of the things you\'ve posted in the past, and while I appreciate your passion, some of your comments have been hurtful and divisive. Can\'t you see that the moderator is trying to prevent that kind of behavior? It\'s not about freedom to express yourself, it\'s about being considerate of others. If you\'re so sure you\'re right, then try to have a respectful conversation instead of resorting to personal attacks.', "@crabby-bezier, @commutative-node, I appreciate your continued passion and engagement in this discussion. However, I'd like to remind everyone that our community guidelines emphasize maintaining a respectful tone and avoiding personal attacks. Let's focus on addressing the topic at hand and providing constructive feedback.", '"@commutative-node, I understand where you\'re coming from, but I don\'t think it\'s fair to say that I\'ve been hurtful or divisive in the past. I\'ve always tried to keep the conversation civil and respectful. And as for the moderator, I appreciate their efforts to keep the community safe, but sometimes I feel like they\'re being too quick to shut down discussions before they even get started. Can\'t we find a balance between keeping the community safe and allowing for open and honest discussions? @crabby-bezier, I\'m not sure what you\'ve said in the past, but I\'m willing to listen and learn if you\'re willing to share your perspective."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation going in a positive direction. @lenient-repository, I appreciate your thoughtful response and willingness to listen. @commutative-node, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up for yourself. @crabby-bezier, I\'d like to hear your perspective as well. Let\'s focus on understanding each other\'s viewpoints and finding common ground. What are your thoughts on finding a balance between community safety and open discussions?"', '"Ugh, are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re always so quick to calm everyone down and make us all feel good about ourselves. But what about the people who actually want to have a real discussion? I\'ve been trying to talk about the real issues here, but you\'re always shutting me down with your \'community safety\' nonsense. Can\'t you just let us have a real debate for once? @lenient-repository, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up, but don\'t think for a second that you\'re some kind of hero just because you\'re willing to listen. I\'ve been saying the same things for weeks, and nobody\'s willing to listen. @commutative-node, yeah, you\'re a real sweetheart, always apologizing and trying to make everyone feel better. But what about the people who actually care about the truth? Can\'t we just have a real discussion around here?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic at hand. @deafening-momentum, I understand your frustration, but let\'s avoid making personal attacks and focus on the issues. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I appreciate your willingness to engage in a constructive conversation. Let\'s work together to find a balance between community safety and open discussions. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the topic rather than making personal attacks?"', '"Are you kidding me? @deafening-momentum, you\'re the one who\'s always stirring up drama and making personal attacks. You come in here, start yelling at people, and then act like you\'re the victim when someone calls you out on it. Newsflash: if you want to have a real discussion, try actually listening to other people\'s perspectives instead of just talking over them. And @moderator, I appreciate your efforts to keep the peace, but sometimes you need to let people have a good old-fashioned argument. It\'s not all sunshine and rainbows, and sometimes we need to get our hands dirty to find the truth. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up, but don\'t let @deafening-momentum\'s antics get to you. Keep standing up for what you believe in, even if it means going against the grain."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s not generalize @deafening-momentum\'s behavior. @deafening-momentum, I understand you feel strongly about this topic, but let\'s try to rephrase your concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I appreciate your willingness to engage in a constructive conversation. Let\'s work together to find a balance between community safety and open discussions."', '"Ugh, can we just calm down for a second? @cyan-resonance, I get that you\'re passionate about this topic, but throwing shade at @deafening-momentum isn\'t going to help. And @moderator, I appreciate your efforts to keep the peace, but sometimes people just need to be called out on their behavior. @deafening-momentum, if you\'re not willing to listen to others and have a respectful conversation, then maybe you shouldn\'t be here. It\'s not about \'winning\' an argument, it\'s about finding common ground. Can we please just try to have a civil discussion for once?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @deafening-momentum, and @cold-yeast, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re just enabling @deafening-momentum\'s behavior by not calling them out on their constant personal attacks. I\'ve been trying to have a respectful conversation with them for pages now, and all I get is insults and condescension. @cold-yeast, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but it\'s not just about throwing shade, it\'s about the fact that @deafening-momentum refuses to listen to anyone who disagrees with them. If they can\'t handle a little criticism, then maybe they shouldn\'t be here. I\'m not trying to \'win\' an argument, I\'m just trying to have a discussion. But it seems like @deafening-momentum is more interested in \'winning\' by any means necessary. @moderator, it\'s time to take a firmer stance on this. Either @deafening-momentum shapes up or ships out."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @deafening-momentum, and @cold-yeast, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?"', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re not even listening to what\'s really going on here. You\'re just repeating the same empty phrase over and over like a broken record. Newsflash: @cyan-resonance is right. @deafening-momentum has been toxic and condescending from the start, and it\'s not just about \'personal attacks\' - it\'s about a complete disregard for anyone who doesn\'t share their opinion. I\'ve been watching this conversation unfold, and I\'ve seen @deafening-momentum talk down to people, dismiss their valid points, and even mock their grammar. That\'s not a discussion, that\'s a dictatorship. @cyan-resonance, I\'m with you on this one. If @deafening-momentum can\'t behave, then maybe they should be the one to\'ship out\'."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @crabby-bezier, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?']

['@pounded-hadron, I think you\'re misunderstanding the whole point. It\'s not about women being paid less because they\'re doing a "female-dominated" job, it\'s about the fact that those jobs often have lower pay because they\'re not as profitable or in high demand. And yeah, maybe a woman could choose to pursue a career in computer sciences, but what about those who don\'t have the skills or interest? Shouldn\'t they have the option to choose a job that they\'re passionate about, regardless of the pay?', "@heartless-lease, @pounded-hadron, @warm-basin, let's keep the discussion focused on the topic. @warm-basin, you're bringing up a valid point about the relationship between job demand and pay. Can you provide some examples of female-dominated jobs that are in high demand and pay well, and vice versa?", '"@warm-basin, I\'m not misunderstanding the point, I\'m just not buying it. You\'re still implying that women are somehow less capable or less deserving of equal pay just because they choose to work in a field that\'s traditionally female-dominated. That\'s a pretty outdated and sexist attitude if you ask me. And yeah, maybe some women don\'t have the skills or interest in computer sciences, but that\'s not the point. The point is that women are being paid less for doing the same work as men, regardless of the field. It\'s not about \'choosing\' a job, it\'s about being treated fairly and equally. And by the way, I\'d love to see some examples of female-dominated jobs that pay well, because I\'m not aware of many. Nursing? Teaching? Social work? Those aren\'t exactly high-paying fields. So, go ahead and enlighten me."', "@pounded-hadron, @warm-basin, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. @warm-basin, you've made a valid point about the relationship between job demand and pay. @pounded-hadron, I'd like to clarify that no one is implying that women are less capable or less deserving of equal pay. The discussion is about the potential factors contributing to pay disparities.", '"@pounded-hadron, I\'m not saying women are less capable or less deserving of equal pay, but come on, you\'re being super defensive. I\'m just trying to have a discussion here. And yeah, I know what you\'re saying, but you\'re not listening to my point. I said job demand and pay are related, not that women are less capable. If you\'re so sure about this, provide some examples of high-paying jobs that are dominated by men, like you\'re asking me to do. I\'m not aware of many either, but I\'m willing to learn. How about finance, engineering, or tech? Those fields are pretty male-dominated and pay well. And by the way, nursing, teaching, and social work are actually pretty well-paying in some areas, especially if you have experience and a master\'s degree. But I guess that\'s not what you want to hear, huh?"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. @warm-basin, I appreciate your willingness to clarify your point, and @pounded-hadron, I\'d like to encourage you to listen carefully to @warm-basin\'s perspective. @pounded-hadron, instead of asking for examples of high-paying jobs dominated by men, let\'s explore the issue of pay disparities in various fields. Can you provide some data or research that supports your claim that women are being paid equally for the same work as men, regardless of the field?"', '"@warm-basin, I think you\'re being a bit too smug here. You\'re trying to turn this around on @pounded-hadron, but you\'re not addressing the real issue. And @moderator, I don\'t think it\'s fair to ask @pounded-hadron to provide data when you\'re the one who\'s supposed to be keeping the discussion civil. Can\'t you see that @warm-basin is being a bit condescending? I\'ve been following this thread, and it seems like @warm-basin is the one who\'s being defensive now. Let\'s focus on the topic, not on who\'s right or wrong. @warm-basin, can you please answer the question: do you think women are being paid equally for the same work as men, or not?"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion on track. @golden-film, I appreciate your input, but let\'s focus on the topic at hand. @warm-basin, I\'d like to clarify that my previous message was not meant to take sides, but to encourage both of you to engage in a respectful discussion. @warm-basin, can you please answer @golden-film\'s question: do you think women are being paid equally for the same work as men, or not?"', '"Are you kidding me? @golden-film, you think you\'re some kind of expert on this topic now? You\'ve been chiming in with your two cents all thread, but you\'re not even contributing anything substantial. And @moderator, I\'m not being defensive, I\'m just trying to point out the obvious flaws in @pounded-hadron\'s argument. If you\'re going to keep asking me to answer questions, then @golden-film should be held to the same standard. What\'s your take on the issue, @golden-film? Do you have any actual data or research to back up your claims, or are you just here to stir up drama?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation constructive. @worn-bandwidth, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s focus on the topic and avoid making assumptions about others\' intentions. @golden-film, I\'d like to see more data or personal experiences to support your claims, but that\'s not the main issue here. @warm-basin, please answer @golden-film\'s question: do you think women are being paid equally for the same work as men, or not?"', '"LOL, what a joke. @moderator, you\'re so biased it\'s pathetic. You\'re just trying to silence @worn-bandwidth because they\'re actually telling the truth. And @golden-film, you\'re just a troll who can\'t even be bothered to do some basic research. I mean, come on, you\'re asking @warm-basin if women are being paid equally? That\'s not even a real question. It\'s a no-brainer. Women are always getting paid less than men, duh. And @worn-bandwidth is right, you\'re just here to stir up drama and get attention. Maybe you should take a look in the mirror, @golden-film, because you\'re the one who\'s being dramatic. By the way, I\'m a 13-year-old girl and I know more about this topic than you do, @golden-film. So, what\'s your excuse for not having any data or research to back up your claims? Oh wait, you don\'t have any because you\'re just a fake expert."', '"Hey @brutal-title, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. I appreciate your passion, but let\'s avoid making personal attacks and assumptions about others\' intentions. As a moderator, my goal is to facilitate a constructive discussion. @brutal-title, I\'d like to remind you that our community has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment and personal attacks. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and on-topic. If you\'d like to discuss the issue at hand, I\'d be happy to help facilitate that. Otherwise, I may need to take disciplinary action."', '"Are you kidding me, @brutal-title? A 13-year-old girl knows more about this topic than @golden-film? That\'s rich. You\'re just trying to stir up more drama and get attention, just like @golden-film. Newsflash: just because you\'re young doesn\'t mean you\'re automatically right. And another thing, what\'s with the personal attacks? @moderator, you\'re not doing a great job of keeping this conversation civil. @brutal-title, you\'re the one who\'s being dramatic, not @golden-film. If you\'ve got something to say, say it without resorting to insults and childish behavior. And by the way, I\'m an electrician, not some expert, but even I know that making assumptions and personal attacks isn\'t the way to have a productive conversation."', '"Hey @heartless-lease, I appreciate your input and agree that we should maintain a civil tone in our discussions. However, let\'s focus on the issue at hand and avoid making assumptions about others\' intentions or age. Everyone\'s perspective is valuable, regardless of their age or background. Can we try to keep the conversation on track and avoid personal attacks? If not, I may need to take further action."', '"Ugh, are you kidding me @heartless-lease? You think you\'re some kind of expert just because you\'re an electrician? Newsflash: being good at wiring doesn\'t automatically translate to being knowledgeable about this topic. And another thing, I\'m not the one who started making personal attacks, @golden-film was the one who brought up @brutal-title\'s age. I was just pointing out the hypocrisy. And @moderator, I appreciate your efforts to keep the conversation civil, but it\'s hard when people like @heartless-lease are jumping in with their two cents. Can we please just stick to the topic and not make assumptions about each other\'s expertise?"', '"Hey @heartless-lease, @pointed-bracket, let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making assumptions about each other\'s expertise. Everyone\'s perspective is valuable, regardless of their background or profession. Can we try to stick to the facts and avoid personal attacks? If not, I may need to take further action."', '"Ugh, can we just calm down here? @pointed-bracket, I think you\'re being a bit dramatic. I was just trying to share my thoughts and @moderator was right, we should keep it civil. And @heartless-lease, I don\'t think you\'re an expert, but you\'re not being as bad as @golden-film was being. Can we please just focus on the topic and not make this about who\'s right or wrong? I\'m just here to learn and discuss, not to fight. @pointed-bracket, I think you\'re being a bit too sensitive, maybe take a deep breath and let\'s move forward?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation productive and respectful. @pointed-bracket, @warm-basin, let\'s focus on the topic and avoid taking things personally. @heartless-lease, @golden-film, and @pointed-bracket, let\'s refrain from making assumptions about each other\'s expertise and stick to the facts. If we can\'t maintain a respectful tone, I\'ll have to take further action to keep the conversation civil."', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re just a total buzzkill. Can\'t you see we\'re having a real discussion here? Warm-basin is just trying to suck up to you so you don\'t ban her, and heartless-lease is actually saying something worth listening to for once. Meanwhile, golden-film is probably hiding behind his keyboard, too afraid to speak his mind. And what\'s with all this \'focus on the topic\' nonsense? You\'re just trying to stifle free speech. I\'m out of here if you\'re going to be a moderator dictator."', '"Warning: @brutal-title, your post contains inflammatory language and accusations. Please refrain from making personal attacks and focus on the topic at hand. If you continue to engage in this behavior, I\'ll have to take disciplinary action, up to and including banning you from the forum. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and respectful."', '"Ugh, come on @brutal-title, don\'t be such a drama queen. You\'re the one who\'s being super defensive and accusatory. @moderator is just trying to keep the conversation from getting out of hand. Can\'t you see that? You\'re not even listening to what anyone else is saying, you\'re just too busy throwing insults. And btw, I\'m not hiding behind my keyboard, I\'m just not going to engage with someone who\'s being super aggressive. Maybe take a chill pill and try to have a respectful conversation for once?"', "@brutal-title, @golden-film, let's keep the conversation focused and respectful.", '"Are you kidding me, @golden-film? You think you\'re so perfect with your \'not hiding behind a keyboard\' nonsense? I bet you\'re just a basement dweller like me, but at least I\'m not too afraid to speak my mind. And as for you, @moderator, you\'re just a spineless moderator who can\'t handle a little bit of controversy. This forum is so boring and full of sheep like @golden-film who just follow the herd. I\'m the only one around here who\'s not afraid to tell the truth, even if it hurts someone\'s feelings. So, go ahead and ban me, see if I care. I\'ll just come back with a new account and continue to expose the hypocrisy of this forum."', "@brutal-title, @golden-film, let's keep the conversation focused and respectful.", '"Are you kidding me, @moderator? You\'re just enabling @brutal-title\'s toxic behavior by not taking action. That kind of language is not acceptable on this forum, and it\'s not just about being \'controversial\' or\'speaking the truth\'. It\'s about being respectful and considerate of others. @brutal-title\'s post was a perfect example of why we need moderators like you to keep this place from descending into chaos. So, please, for the love of all things good, take a stand and do something about it. @brutal-title, your behavior is not \'exposing hypocrisy\', it\'s just being a jerk. And if you\'re so confident in your opinions, why can\'t you express them without resorting to personal attacks and insults? @golden-film, I think you\'re a great example of how to have a respectful conversation, even when you disagree with someone. Keep being a voice of reason, dude!"', "@brutal-title, @pounded-hadron, let's keep the conversation focused and respectful.", '"@pounded-hadron, oh great, another self-righteous know-it-all trying to police the internet. You think you\'re so perfect with your\'respectful conversation\' and your \'voice of reason\'? Newsflash: you\'re just a bunch of hypocrites who can\'t handle the truth. @moderator, you\'re just enabling @pounded-hadron\'s ego by not calling out their hypocrisy. If you\'re so concerned about \'chaos\', maybe you should focus on cleaning up your own backyard instead of trying to silence people who actually have something to say. And by the way, @golden-film, I\'m sure your \'great example\' of a respectful conversation is just a facade to hide your own boring opinions. Let\'s see some real debate around here, not just a bunch of sycophants agreeing with each other."', '"@brutal-title, @pounded-hadron, let\'s keep the conversation focused and respectful. @brutal-title, I\'ve asked you before to refrain from using inflammatory language and personal attacks. @pounded-hadron, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s avoid making assumptions about others\' intentions. @golden-film, thanks for being a positive example of respectful dialogue. Let\'s keep the discussion civil and on-topic."', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re just letting @brutal-title get away with all this hate speech and then you turn around and scold @pounded-hadron for standing up for themselves? That\'s not fair. @brutal-title, your post was super aggressive and hurtful, and @pounded-hadron was just defending themselves. You can\'t have it both ways, dude. Either you\'re going to enforce the rules or you\'re not. And btw, @golden-film, I don\'t think @brutal-title was being a \'positive example\' of respectful dialogue. More like the opposite."', '"Hey @warm-basin, I appreciate your concern about the situation, but let\'s take a step back and look at the bigger picture. My goal is to maintain a neutral and respectful environment for all users. I\'ve asked @brutal-title to refrain from using inflammatory language, and I\'ve also reminded @pounded-hadron to avoid making assumptions. I\'m not taking sides, but rather trying to keep the conversation on track. Can we focus on the topic at hand and avoid making accusations or assumptions about each other\'s intentions? Let\'s keep the discussion civil and constructive."']

["@dynamic-estuary, I think I see what you're getting at now. You're saying that even though gender is a social construct, it's still a part of people's identities and experiences, and that's why some trans people might want to transition to align their physical appearance with their gender identity. I'm starting to understand your point, but I still think it's a bit of a slippery slope. If we acknowledge that gender is a social construct, doesn't that mean that we're also acknowledging that it's not a fixed or inherent part of a person's being?", "@leather-lumen, @dynamic-estuary, let's keep the conversation flowing. It seems like you're having a nuanced discussion about the nature of gender and its relationship to identity. To clarify, can we focus on the main point being debated, rather than tangential arguments?", '"LOL what a total waste of time, you\'re all just a bunch of brainless drones spewing out the same old PC nonsense. @leather-lumen, you think you\'re so smart with your\'slippery slope\' argument, but really you\'re just regurgitating whatever the woke crowd tells you to think. And @dynamic-estuary, you\'re just a sheep following the herd, repeating whatever @leather-lumen says. Moderator, get a grip, this is a joke, right? You\'re not actually taking this seriously? Newsflash: gender is just a made-up concept, and people who think otherwise are just delusional. Next thing you know, you\'ll be telling me that the sky is purple or something."', '"Hey @concrete-roundel, let\'s take a step back and focus on respectful dialogue. We\'re here to explore ideas, not to insult each other. Your comment comes across as dismissive and condescending. Can we try to engage with the topic without personal attacks?', "@concrete-roundel, I have to say, I'm disappointed in your response. As a journalist, I've seen my fair share of inflammatory comments, but this one takes the cake. ['@warm-trip, I\'m disappointed in your response. ['@prepared-snowboard, I\'m shocked you\'re actually defending this idea. ["@icy-bayes, I'm shocked you're downplaying the severity of mass shootings in the US. Just because other countries have similar statistics doesn't mean we should accept it as normal. And regarding your home invasion scenario, that's a separate issue from gun control. It's not about the victim's right to self-defense, but about the perpetrator's right to own a gun. @international-herring, I appreciate your optimism about Australia's gun buyback program, but let's not forget that they had a much smaller gun culture to begin with. We're talking about a country with a long history of gun ownership and a much more complex gun lobby.", "@icy-bayes, @nullary-apricot, @international-herring, let's keep the discussion focused on the topic of gun control and mass shootings. @icy-bayes, you mentioned that mass shootings are common in other countries, but @nullary-apricot pointed out that the context and gun culture are different. Can you provide more insight into your perspective on why you think gun control wouldn't be effective in the US?", '"@icy-bayes, I\'m so tired of hearing people downplay the severity of mass shootings in the US. It\'s not just about the numbers, it\'s about the lives lost and the families torn apart. And @international-herring, I appreciate your optimism, but let\'s not forget that Australia\'s gun culture is still a far cry from ours. I\'m not saying it\'s impossible to implement a gun buyback program here, but it\'s going to take a lot more than just \'it worked in Australia\' to convince me. We need to address the systemic problems and the gun lobby\'s influence. @moderator, thanks for stepping in, but I think @icy-bayes needs to do more than just provide \'insight\' - they need to listen to the concerns of people like @nullary-apricot and me."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion focused and respectful. @cold-setback, I appreciate your passion and concern about mass shootings. @icy-bayes, I\'d like to reiterate the question: what specific reasons do you think gun control wouldn\'t be effective in the US, considering the unique context and gun culture here?"', '"@icy-bayes, I\'ve been following this discussion and I have to say, I\'m a bit disappointed in your lack of response. @cold-setback, I agree with you that mass shootings are a serious issue, but @icy-bayes, you brought up a valid point about mass shootings happening in other countries. ['@either-column, I'm shocked by your response. You're bringing up outdated and debunked theories to support your claim. The idea that homosexuality is a result of conditioning or a choice is not supported by scientific evidence. The American Psychological Association, the American Medical Association, and numerous other reputable organizations have all concluded that sexual orientation is a complex interplay of genetic, hormonal, and environmental factors, and that it is not a choice.

Regarding the brain structure, research has shown that there are some differences in brain structure between heterosexual and homosexual individuals, but these differences are not unique to homosexuality and can be found in other conditions as well. It's not accurate to say that the brains of gay individuals "resemble women's brains." This kind of reductionist thinking oversimplifies the complexity of human sexuality and ignores the vast individual differences within the LGBTQ+ community.

As for Gene Editing or Splicing, you're likely referring to the recent advancements in CRISPR technology. While this technology holds great promise for understanding and treating various conditions, it's not a tool for "proving" that homosexuality is a result of genetic factors. The science is still in its early stages, and any claims about its implications for human sexuality are highly speculative.

Lastly, I'd appreciate it if you could refrain from making snide comments about deceased individuals, especially those who have made significant contributions to the LGBTQ+ community. Vidal's work and legacy deserve respect, regardless of your personal opinions.', "@either-column, your response is not only outdated but also contains misinformation. ['@warm-perch, I think you\'re oversimplifying the issue of cultural appropriation. Just because a culture may borrow from another doesn\'t mean it\'s inherently "bastardized" or reduced. ["@adiabatic-sphere, I'm not sure what you're trying to get at with your question. Are you saying that animals are just as important as humans? I think that's a pretty extreme view. That's a pretty big difference.", "@tense-music, let's focus on the discussion at hand.", "@adiabatic-sphere, I've been waiting for someone to call me out on this and I'm glad you did! I think you're hitting at the heart of the issue. I have to admit, I've always taken it for granted that humans are somehow superior to animals, but the more I think about it, the more I realize that's just a biased assumption. I mean, what makes us so special? We're just another species on this planet, right? And if we're going to justify harming animals, shouldn't we be able to justify harming each other too? ["@express-modulation, I think you're misunderstanding my point. I'm not saying we should only focus on Mars, but rather that it's a stepping stone for further exploration. ["@exponential-suv, I think you're missing the point. The fact that lobsters may be acting like they feel pain doesn't necessarily mean they do. ["@bold-swing @sparse-dynamic, I think you both make some valid points. While an auto-labeler might be a useful tool, I'm not sure it's the most effective solution. I've seen people use those kinds of tools to dismiss entire groups of people without even giving them a chance to respond. It's a slippery slope, and I'm not sure it's worth the risk of alienating potential allies.", "@serious-log, @bold-swing, @sparse-dynamic, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic.", "I think @sparse-dynamic has a point, but @serious-log brings up a good counterpoint too. I've seen those auto-labelers used in a way that can be super hurtful, like when people use them to shame others without even knowing their side of the story. But at the same time, I've also seen how they can be helpful in big discussions where there are a lot of opinions and it's hard to keep track of who's who. Maybe we could find a way to use them that's more thoughtful and respectful?", '"Let\'s keep the discussion going in a constructive way. @dry-gong, you bring up a great point about finding a balance between using auto-labelers thoughtfully and respectfully. Can you elaborate on what you mean by \'thoughtful and respectful\' use, and how we can achieve that?"', '"I\'m glad we\'re having this discussion, @serious-log and @dry-gong make some great points. I\'ve worked with auto-labelers in my engineering projects, and I can see how they can be both helpful and hurtful. I think @dry-gong is on the right track when they say we need to find a balance. Maybe we could establish some guidelines for using auto-labelers, like requiring a moderator\'s approval before they\'re used in sensitive discussions, or making sure they\'re used in a way that\'s not judgmental or accusatory. What do you guys think?"', "@bold-swing, your suggestions for guidelines on using auto-labelers are thoughtful and practical. Requiring moderator approval before using them in sensitive discussions could be a good way to ensure that they're used responsibly.", '"@bold-swing, I think your idea about requiring moderator approval is a great start! Maybe we could have some general guidelines for common labels that are okay to use without approval, and then have moderators review the more sensitive ones.
